notes: A NON-retryable mid-turn API failure (mock-gemini-5xx returns 400 INVALID_ARGUMENT on the main streamGenerateContent turn; a 429/500/503 is retryable and writes NO transcript line, so it cannot exercise this path) makes gemini-cli record a type:"error" MessageRecord in ~/.gemini/tmp/<project>/chats/session-*.jsonl (PR #13300), followed by a terminal type:"info" "This request failed." notice. SETTLED by the live recording under the #665-fixed parser: parseError maps the type:"error" line to turn_done (and parseInfo maps the "This request failed" terminal notice the same way, #676), so the aborted turn settles the rollout session working→ready instead of sticking in working. The recording confirms the correct contract: the rollout session reaches working on the prompt and returns to ready when the error lands (events.jsonl reason "agent finished turn → ready"). SESSION IDENTITY (two-tier): a transient proc-<PID> presession is born ready (presession_birth); the rollout session reconciles with a different session_id keyed on the transcript header (session_birth, new_session, order-independent of the presession); turn_start/turn_end_error anchor to the rollout session, never the presession. Recorded via the interactive driver (gemini -y under tmux: send + 15s sleep — NOT wait_turn, which would time out since the turn never completes a normal reply). The failure inducer (GOOGLE_GEMINI_BASE_URL) is honored only on the API-key path (issue #15430). Observations: an aborted turn bills nothing, so model is empty and cost/tokens are zero (relaxed from the basic-turn shape).

text: A short-lived presession (heap-bump/pre-rollout proc) appears in ready first; absorbed here so the real rollout phases below don't false-match it
The real rollout session reconciles (a different session_id keyed on the transcript header), born ready — new_session pins it off the presession regardless of which appears first
User prompt drives the rollout session ready → working before the API error lands
The type:"error" line drives the rollout session working → ready: the #665-fixed parser maps it to turn_done so the aborted turn settles instead of sticking in working
